original: NASA launches Galaxy-C

Wallal expedition of astronomers and local Nyangumarta people south of Broome, Australia, photograph total solar eclipse provingAlbert Einstein's Theory of Relativity, that space is curved by showing deflection of starlight round the sun
J. R. R. Tolkien's "The Hobbit" is published by George Allen and Unwin in London
Teiichi Igarashi, Japanese mountaineer and oldest person to climb Mt Fuji at age 99, born in Japan
2,251 turn out to see Expos play NY Mets at Shea Stadium
Antonio Abertondo swims English Channel both ways (44 miles)
East German swimmer Silke Hörner sets world record 2:26.71 to win the 200m breaststroke gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; first of 2 gold (4 x 100m medley relay)
Hungarian swimmer Tamás Darnyi sets world record 4:14.75 to win the 400m individual medley at the Seoul Olympics; also wins 200m I/M gold for the medley double
Fossil of Dickinsonia, “the Holy Grail of palaeontology” proven to be oldest known animal fossil, 558 million years old from White Sea, Russia
Suriname swimmer Anthony Nesty wins 100m butterfly at the Seoul Olympics; Suriname's first gold; first black to win individual Olympic swim gold medal; thwartsMatt Biondi's attempt at 7 gold medals
Discovery of the first brainless animal that sleeps, the jellyfish Cassiopea, research published in "Current Biology" by Caltech scientists
Charles Upham, New Zealand soldier, only combatant soldier to be awarded the Victoria Cross twice, born in Christchurch, New Zealand (d. 1994)[1]
Sykuro Manabe, Japanese-American meteorologist (2021 Nobel Prize for Physics for pioneering work on climate change), born in Ehime, Japan
Soviet spacecraft Zond 5 lands in the Indian ocean after a six-day flight - first successful circumlunar mission with two onboard tortoises surviving the trip
Richard Todd of NY Jets completes 42 passes in a game (NFL record)
USA Basketball announces "Dream Team" for the 1992 Olympics
Galileo mission terminated by sending the probe into Jupiter's atmosphere, where it is crushed by the pressure at the lower altitudes
Genomic study finding Australian Aboriginal oldest known civilisation on earth published in "Nature"

Charles "Chuck" Jones, American animator and cartoonist best known for his work with Warner Bros. (Bugs Bunny, Daffy Duck, Wile E. Coyote, Road Runner, Porky Pig), born in Spokane, Washington (d. 2002)
AFL New York Jets Steve O'Neal (23) kicks 98 yard punt, the longest professional American football punt in Denver against the Broncos
Soviet space probe Luna 16 leaves the Moon
New Orleans Saints Mel Gray returns kickoff 101 yards for a touchdown
The Hindu milk miracle occurs: statues of the Hindu God Ganesh begin drinking milk when spoonfuls were placed near their mouths
Deep Space 1 flies within 2,200 km of Comet Borrelly.
US Mariner 10 makes 2nd fly-by of Mercury
Three genetic studies published in "Nature" conclude all non-Africans descended from one migration out of Africa 50-80,000 years ago
NY Sun runs famous "Yes, Virginia there is a Santa Claus" editorial
In his last fight, undefeated world heavyweight boxing championRocky MarcianoKOs light heavyweightArchie Moorein the 9th round at Yankee Stadium, NYC
Gustav Holst, English composer (Planets), born in Cheltenham, Gloucestershire, England (d. 1934)
The North American XB-70 Valkyrie, the world's first Mach 3 bomber, makes its maiden flight from Palmdale, California
Louis Jolliet, Canadian explorer (explored the origins of the Mississippi River helped by Native Americans), born in Quebec, Canada (d. 1700)
Jan and Nicolaas van der Heyden patent the fire hose
Battle of Preston Pans: Bonnie Prince Charles beats British government army
Antoine de Beauterne announces he has killed the Beast of Gévaudan, but was later proved wrong by more attacks
Frank Duryea drives the 1st American-made gas propelled vehicle
The Great Hurricane of 1938 makes landfall on Long Island in New York, killing an estimated 500-700 people
US Senate confirms Sandra Day O'Conner to Supreme Court (99-0)
Construction of the Burj Dubai starts
McDonald's announces plans to "drastically" reduce plastic in its Happy Meals by 2025 (these meals make it one of largest toy distributors in the world)
New York Yankees set dubious MLB record, stranding 20 on base;Mickey Mantlehits a 500' plus homer but rival Boston Red Sox win 13-9 at Fenway Park
Jimmy Hendrixchanges spelling of his name to "Jimi"
Michael Spinks beatsLarry Holmesin 15 to become Heavyweight Boxing Champion
Heike Kamerlingh Onnes, Dutch physicist (liquid helium, Nobel 1913), born in Groningen, Netherlands (d. 1926)
Learie Constantine, West Indian cricket all-rounder, lawyer and politician (UK's first black peer), born in Diego Martin, Trinidad (d. 1971)[1]
Bjarni Tryggvason, Icelandic-Canadian astronaut (STS 85), born in Reykjavik, Iceland (d. 2022)
Lynette Love, American taekwondo player (World C'ship gold heavyweight 1987, 1991), born in Chicago, Illinois
Trugoy the Dove [David Jolicoeur], American rapper and producer (De La Soul - "Me Myself and I"), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 2023)
English KingRichard Ithe Lion hearted, captured by Leopold V, Duke of Austria
Nathan Hale, American rebel spy is arrested by British militia in NYC
Cecil Chubb buys the English prehistoric monument Stonehenge for £6,600
According toJoseph SmithJr., the angel Moroni gave him a record of gold plates, one-third of which Joseph translated into The Book of Mormon
Johann Ostermeyer patents flashbulb
RKO film comedy "Room Service", starring the Marx Brothers, based on Allen Boretz and John Murray's play, premieres in NYC
Five inches of rain falls on NYC
Facebook CEOMark Zuckerbergand his wife Priscilla Chan pledge $3 billion to medical research to "cure, prevent or manage all diseases by the end of the century"
WHO DirectorTedros Ghebreyesussays countries representing 2/3 of the world's population have joined its COVAX vaccine-distribution initiative to deliver 2 billion doses by end 2021
Bernard Silver, American co-inventor of the barcode, born in Pennsylvania (d. 1963)[1]
Donald A. Glaser, American physicist (Nobel  Prize,1960), born in Cleveland, Ohio (d. 2013)
Richard Childress, American NASCAR HOF owner (RCR: winner Winston Cup Series 1986, 87, 90, 91, 93, 94; Xfinity Series 2001, 03, 06, 07, 13, 19), born in Winston-Salem, North Carolina
Richard J Hieb, astronaut (STS 39, 49, 65), born in Jamestown, North Dakota
